title: Robinhood Q3 2024 Earnings: Key Takeaways
text: Robinhood delivered strong Q3 results with significant growth across multiple metrics. Financial Performance: Revenue $637 million, up 36% year-over-year—the second-highest in company history. Adjusted EBITDA $268 million, nearly doubled with 42% margins (13-point expansion). Assets Under Custody: Record $152 billion, up 76%. Net Deposits: $10 billion for the third consecutive quarter. Growth Drivers: Transaction-based revenues surged 72%, driven by strong customer engagement. Options contracts reached a new record while equity and crypto volumes jumped 65% and over 100% respectively. Product Momentum: Management unveiled three major initiatives targeting active traders: Index Options with competitive $0.50 pricing ($0.35 for Gold members), Futures contracts at $0.75 per contract ($0.50 for subscribers), Robinhood Legend desktop platform which filled 1,000 beta slots in 45 seconds. Gold Card Expansion: The credit card program showed exceptional demand with a waitlist approaching 2 million. Early data indicates approximately 95% retention after first transactions. Forward Outlook: Management anticipates Q4 adjusted OpEx/SBC near the upper end of guidance while maintaining disciplined cost management alongside continued growth investments.
